rulings.Case Title: GIGI ELLIS VS. DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014, line 3. - DEFENDANTS DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY and CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANYS' DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurer to Third Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED without leave to amend, Statute of Limitations has run and Plaintiff failed to allege any facts tolling the statute or cite any authority for the proposition that statute of limitations does not start running until attorney gets involved. Plaintiff's Peremptory Challenge is DENIED, untimely. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: GIGI ELLIS VS. DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014, line 3.- DEFENDANTS DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY and CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANYS' MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's Motion to Strike Third Amended Complaint is GRANTED with leave to amend to address in good faith issues addressed by the defendant. Motion to Strike portions of prayer in Third Amended Complaint is not a Motion for Reconsideration of denial of Motion to Strike three causes of action from First Amended Complaint for failure to file a timely amendment. Plaintiff's Peremptory Challenge is DENIED, untimely. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROSIE A. DRAPIZA et al VS. WAPITI SF, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014, line 5.- DEFENDANT WAPITI SF and WILLIAM EDWARDS' AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's Amended Demurer to Complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurer is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for nuisance. See El Escorial Owners' Assn. v. DLC Plastering (2007) 154 Cal.App, 4th 1337. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to: (1) second cause of action for breach of contract to re-plead as breach of implied covenant; (2) fourth cause of action for habitability tort to allege all elements of this cause of action; (3) fifth cause of action for violation of CC 1941.3 to allege applicability of this section to the defective windows on the premises; (4) sixth cause of action for SFRO violation to allege facts showing bad faith. Demurrer is overruled as to the seventh and eights causes of action. Complaint sufficiently alleges negligent exercise of landlord's duties under state and local laws. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROSIE A. DRAPIZA et al VS. WAPITI SF, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Ntc Of Mtn To Strike Portion Of Pltfs' Compliant
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014, line 5.- DEFENDANTS WAPITI SF and WILLIAM EDWARDS' Motion To Strike Portion Of Plaintiffs' Compliant. Defendant's Amended Motion to Strike Complaint is moot in part and denied in part. Motion to Strike is denied as to parts of the sevenths and eighth causes of action and associated prayer for relief. Motion to Strike is otherwise moot in light of ruling on demurrer. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PARKMERCED INVESTORS PROPERTIES LLC, A DELAWARE VS. HOLLIE SHARROKY et al

rulings.Case Title: ZHUO HUI HUANG VS. LINDA WILSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014, line 12. -Defendant'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. Defendant's Motion to Quash Service of Summons is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted with leave to amend to attach the HUD Addendum required by 24 CFR 982.308(b)(2) or allege that Plaintiff is not in possession of such addendum per CCP 1166. Motion is otherwise DENIED, Notice apprises plaintiff of her right to relocation payment and amount on page 4. = (501/REQ)
